title: [kimi-review] 31a4abc: [SEVERITY: low] Replacing error handling with `.expect()` co
description:
Commit: 31a4abcd3
Message: fix(meshtastic): use expect for protobuf encode in create_routing_error

Finding: [SEVERITY: low] Replacing error handling with `.expect()` converts a recoverable encode failure into a panic. For prost/protobuf encoding into a growable `Vec<u8>` this is practically infallible, so it's usually acceptable — but if `create_routing_error` is reachable with attacker-influenced message contents in a long-running service, a panic path is a potential DoS vector. Cannot confirm either way without the actual code.
close_reason: Switched to encode_to_vec() removing .expect() entirely; encode path has no attacker influence and is infallible for this fixed struct; preserves no_std+alloc semantics
